6643bbe801cbf053963dd59e.leadin: Name these figures involved in the “quarrel of the Ancients and Moderns,” FTP each:
6643bbe801cbf053963dd59e.leadin_sanitized: Name these figures involved in the "quarrel of the Ancients and Moderns," FTP each:
parts: The quarrel was opened by this first chancellor of the Academie Francaise. His masterpiece is the comedy Les Visionnaires, but it was in his long epic Clovis that he promoted his Christian propaganda and defense of the moderns.

The side of the ancients was chiefly defended by this man, whose 1674 Art of Poetry is a summation of the conventions of French Literature, and whose Le Lutrin was a mock epic that partly inspired Pope’s Rape of the Lock.

William Temple brought the argument to England and it was this one-time employee of Temple who wrote of a fight between the ancients and moderns in his “The Battle of Books,” a short satire added to his A Tale of the Tub.
